key_insight: Diverse reasoning paths and step-to-step transitions (“thinking schemata”) are strongly linked to better large-language-model math reasoning, and increasing this diversity improves performance.
engineering_relevance: For practical LLM-based math systems, encouraging varied solution trajectories during training and inference can make models more robust and better at recovering from early mistakes.
method: Identify two diversity dimensions: reasoning transitions and answer-candidate paths.
Use Diverse Schemata Policy Optimization (DiScO) to first make schemata explicit, then reinforce diverse reasoning.
Promote diverse inference-time reasoning to widen solution exploration.
evidence_signal: Across multiple mathematical reasoning benchmarks, DiScO consistently outperformed standard group relative policy optimization, and human annotations showed better recovery from incorrect initial attempts.
watch_item: The approach adds complexity in training and inference, so gains may depend on reliable schemata modeling and careful control of diversity without destabilizing correctness.
paper_title: Diverse Thinking Schemata Elicit Better Reasoning in Large Language Models
